def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd; import numpy as np; import ta
    short_window = params['short_window']
    long_window = params['long_window']
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    rsi_threshold = params['rsi_threshold']
    df['short_ma'] = df['close'].rolling(window=short_window).mean()
    df['long_ma'] = df['close'].rolling(window=long_window).mean()
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=rsi_window)
    signals = np.where((df['short_ma'] > df['long_ma']) & (df['rsi'] > rsi_threshold), 1,
                       np.where((df['short_ma'] < df['long_ma']) & (df['rsi'] < rsi_threshold), -1, 0))
    return pd.Series(signals, index=df.index).astype(int)
rationale: GBP/JPY exhibits trend reversal behavior when short-term MA crosses long-term MA confirmed by RSI overbought/oversold conditions.
